relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: After the promise had taken flesh, Abraham was tested, and he answered with presence: Here I am.
The call reached into the deepest bond, asking him to walk toward Moriah with Isaac, the beloved son of promise.
Abraham rose early and began the journey with wood, fire, knife, servants, and son.
On the third day he lifted his eyes and saw the place from afar.
He told the young men to wait while he and the boy went to bow down and return.
Isaac carried the wood, Abraham carried fire and knife, and the two walked together into the test.
Isaac spoke: My father. Abraham answered: Here I am, my son. The question came: where is the lamb?
Abraham answered that the Divine would see and provide, and the two continued together.
At the place, Abraham built the altar, arranged the wood, and bound Isaac.
The test reached the edge of the impossible moment.
The messenger of the Eternal called from heaven, and Abraham again answered: Here I am.
The hand was stopped. The test ended not in loss, but in the revelation that the son was not to be taken.
Abraham lifted his eyes and saw the ram caught in the thicket, provision appearing where the question had been.
He named the place The Eternal Will See and Provide, because on the mountain provision was seen.
The messenger called a second time from heaven.
The oath was sworn by the Divine self, because Abraham had not withheld the beloved son.
Blessing and multiplication were spoken again: stars of heaven, sand of the shore, gates possessed.
Through Abraham’s seed, all nations of the earth would be blessed, because he listened to the voice.
Abraham returned to the young men, and they went together to Beersheba.
After the test, news came of Nahor’s household, quietly preparing the path toward Rebekah.
The genealogy began to unfold through Nahor’s sons.
More names were given in the family line.
Bethuel fathered Rebekah, and the next bridge in the promise line appeared.
The wider household line continued through Reumah and her children.
